title: Effects of a prenatal mindfulness program on longitudinal changes in stress, anxiety, depression, and mother-infant bonding of women with a tendency to perinatal mood and anxiety disorder: a randomized controlled trial
headline: Effects of a prenatal mindfulness program on longitudinal changes in stress, anxiety, depression, and mother-i
summary: A randomized controlled trial of mindfulness / meditation examined stress & anxiety. Despite this, there was no significant difference between the groups in terms of the quality of mother-infant bonding.
keyFindings: Despite this, there was no significant difference between the groups in terms of the quality of mother-infant bonding.
limitations: The self-reported stress, pregnancy-related anxiety, and depression were assessed before and after the intervention and at 36 weeks of gestation.
At 2 and 4 months postpartum, all participants provided self-reported their levels of stress, depression, and quality of mother-infant bonding.
Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.
The practice studied (mindfulness / meditation) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
